soft: {{name}}, you’re not simply a drama queen/king; you’re the whole royal family.
{{name}}, did the middle of my sentence interrupt the beginning of yours?
{{name}}, don’t worry, the first 40 years of childhood are always the hardest.
{{name}}, have a nice day… elsewhere.
{{name}}, is your name Wi-Fi? Because I’m not feeling a connection.
{{name}}, you’re the reason this country has to put directions on shampoo bottles.
{{name}}, you’re so annoying, you could make a Happy Meal cry.
{{name}}, you should really come with a warning label.
{{name}}, you must have a Ph.D. in stupidology.
{{name}}, you are like a software update; every time I see you, I immediately think, 'Not now.'
{{name}}, I gave out all my trophies a while ago, but here’s a participation award.
{{name}}, you’re just like a broken pencil—totally pointless.
{{name}}, you’re the reason that tubes of toothpaste have instructions on them.
{{name}}, uhoh! I smell smoke… are you thinking too hard again?
{{name}}, congrats on getting your PhD in annoyance.
{{name}}, you’re like a grey sprinkle in a funfetti cake.
{{name}}, grab a straw because you suck.
{{name}}, I like the way you comb your hair; it’s impressive how you’re able to hide the horns.
{{name}}, you see that door? I’d love it if you were on the other side of it.
{{name}}, you’re as useful as a screen door on a submarine.
{{name}}, poof, be gone, your breath is too strong; I don’t want to be mean, but you need Listerine.
{{name}}, don’t feel bad, don’t feel blue, Frankenstein was as ugly as you.
{{name}}, I’m not trying to make fun of you, but you can’t even count higher than number two.
{{name}}, it’s not my fault, it’s everyone’s opinion; I’m pretty cool, and you’re just a minion.
{{name}}, roses are red, violets are blue, I thought I was ugly until I met you!
{{name}}, roses are red, monsters are green, look in the mirror, you’ll see what I mean.
{{name}}, your game’s a little rusty, and your jokes feel dusty.
{{name}}, you’re loud on Snap, quiet in crowds—but big energy dies without Wi-Fi around.
{{name}}, you’re a human form of Wednesday morning: unwanted, not needed and totally boring.
{{name}}, your rhymes are straight whack and you have stinky breath, looks like you need a Tic Tac.
{{name}}, you talk like a boss, yet you floss no gloss.
{{name}}, you brag non-stop, but your skills are a flop.
{{name}}, your memory is a fleeting breeze, a forgotten face with such great ease.
{{name}}, you talk a big game, but you play it all lame.
{{name}}, you shine like a star… from very afar.
{{name}}, you try to outshine, but miss every time.
{{name}}, you swear you’re iconic—results say ironic.
{{name}}, you are built on delusion and fueled by confusion.
medium: {{name}}, you have a face that would make onions cry.
{{name}}, I consider you my Sun. Now, please get 93 million miles away from here.
{{name}}, if laughter is the best medicine, your face must be curing the world.
{{name}}, you are the human version of cramps.
{{name}}, you haven’t changed since the last time I saw you. You really should.
{{name}}, if ignorance is bliss, you must be the happiest person on Earth.
{{name}}, I love what you’ve done with your hair. How’d you get it to come out of your nose like that?
{{name}}, I never forget a person’s face, but I’ll be happy to make an exception in your situation.
{{name}}, mirrors can’t talk. Lucky for you, they can’t laugh either.
{{name}}, everyone is allowed to act stupid once in a while, but you’re really abusing the privilege.
{{name}}, let’s play horse. I’ll be the front, and you can be yourself.
{{name}}, I didn’t mean to offend you, but I’ll take the additional perk.
{{name}}, you just might be why the middle finger was invented in the first place.
{{name}}, oh, you’re talking to me? I thought you only did that behind my back.
{{name}}, I’d smack you, but I’m against animal abuse.
{{name}}, it’s hilarious how you try to fit your entire vocabulary into one sentence.
{{name}}, I don’t want to rain on your parade! I’d rather summon a typhoon.
{{name}}, you can’t imagine how much happiness you can bring… by leaving the room.
{{name}}, when I see you coming, I get pre-annoyed. I figure it’s smart to give myself a head start.
{{name}}, if I had a dollar every time you shut up, I would give it back as a thank you.
{{name}}, you are a pizza burn on the roof of the world’s mouth.
{{name}}, I’m not saying you’re ugly, but if you were a scarecrow, birds would avoid you.
{{name}}, honey, the only thing bothering me is placed between your ears.
{{name}}, Earth is full. Go somewhere else.
{{name}}, the people who tolerate you daily are the real heroes.
{{name}}, you have your entire life to be an idiot. Why not take today off?
{{name}}, how many licks till I get to the interesting part of this conversation?
{{name}}, why are you rolling your eyes? Looking for your brain?
{{name}}, let me tell you. If I don’t answer you the first time, what makes you think the next 25 will work?
{{name}}, it’s sad what happened to your face. Oh, wait, that’s how it’s always looked?
{{name}}, I’m listening. I just need a minute to process so much stupid information at once.
{{name}}, when I look at you, I think to myself, ‘Where have you been my whole life? And can you go back there?’
{{name}}, I would agree with you, but then we’d both be wrong.
{{name}}, you look like something that came out of a slow cooker.
{{name}}, you’re so fake, even Barbie is jealous.
{{name}}, I suggest you do a little soul-searching—you may actually find one.
{{name}}, stupidity isn’t a crime, so you’re free to go.
{{name}}, the truth will set you free: you’re the worst. Okay, you’re free to go.
{{name}}, you remind me of the end pieces of a loaf of bread—nobody wants you.
{{name}}, hey, don’t stand too close to the fire. Plastic melts, you know.
{{name}}, I’ll never forget the first time we met, but I promise I’ll keep trying.
{{name}}, if idiots could fly, your house would be an airport.
{{name}}, you bring everyone so much joy, especially when you leave a room.
{{name}}, you say I look ugly today? Good, I was trying to look like you.
{{name}}, you’re dumber than a rock. At least a rock can hold a door open. What can you do?
{{name}}, don’t worry about me. Just worry about your eyebrows.
{{name}}, I promise I’m not insulting you, I’m just describing you.
{{name}}, your face looks like something I’d draw with my left hand.
{{name}}, look on the bright side, if genius skips a generation, your kids will be absolutely brilliant.
{{name}}, if I throw a stick for you, will you leave?
{{name}}, I am unwilling to have a battle of wits with an unarmed opponent like yourself.
{{name}}, the closest you’ll ever come to a brainstorm is a light drizzle.
{{name}}, you look like a “before” photo.
{{name}}, hurting you is the last thing I want to do, but it’s still on the list.
{{name}}, you’re like the human version of athlete’s foot—annoying and hard to get rid of.
{{name}}, oops, my bad. I could’ve sworn I was dealing with an adult.
{{name}}, you’re the reason gene pools need lifeguards.
{{name}}, you’re like a grey cloud. It’s a beautiful day when you disappear.
{{name}}, light travels faster than sound, which is why you seemed bright until you spoke.
{{name}}, I’ve seen clowns like you before, but I always had to pay an admission fee.
{{name}}, good story, but in what chapter do you shut up?
{{name}}, give me a minute. I’m trying to think of an insult simple enough for you to understand!
{{name}}, if your brain were dynamite, there wouldn’t be enough to blow your hat off.
{{name}}, every time I think you can’t get any dumber, you always prove me wrong.
{{name}}, sorry, sarcasm falls out of my mouth like stupidity falls out of yours.
{{name}}, your secrets are always safe with me! I never even listen when you tell me.
{{name}}, hey, your village called. They want their idiot back!
{{name}}, why don’t you check out eBay and see if they have a life for sale?
{{name}}, so, a thought crossed your mind? That must have been a long and lonely journey for you.
{{name}}, don’t feel bad. A lot of people have no talent.
{{name}}, I thought I had the flu, but then I realized your face makes me sick to my stomach.
{{name}}, I thought you were attractive when we first met, then you just had to open your mouth.
{{name}}, can you give me the name of your stylist or barber? I need to know where not to go.
{{name}}, you have a face for radio.
{{name}}, even Bob Ross would call you a mistake.
{{name}}, they say true beauty is on the inside. For your sake, I hope that’s true.
{{name}}, if ugly were a day, you’d be an entire year.
{{name}}, your brain is kind of like a biscuit that’s not done in the middle.
{{name}}, your face could scare the crap out of a toilet.
{{name}}, your fashion sense is quite a mystery, did you get dressed in a state of emergency?
{{name}}, your hairstyle, it’s a work of art! If the goal was to scare, you’ve played your part.
{{name}}, you fake that confidence, are loud but hollow—every thought’s a quote you borrow.
{{name}}, you roast with rage, not with wit—but that’s not skill, that’s just spit.
{{name}}, you think you’re fire, but you’re just smoke—all that talk and you’re still broke.
{{name}}, you’re always giving advice like a sage, yet you live like you’re in a cage.
{{name}}, your sense of humor, it’s a little thin, maybe it got lost in your double chin.
{{name}}, I have five fingers and the middle one is for you.
{{name}}, I’m the type of person to laugh at mistakes, so sorry if I laugh at your face.
savage: {{name}}, I look at you and think, ‘Two billion years of evolution, for this?’
{{name}}, I am jealous of all the people who have never met you.
{{name}}, I was thinking about you today. It reminded me to take out the trash.
{{name}}, when you were born, the doctors probably threw you out of the window, but the window threw you back.
{{name}}, were you born this dumb, or did you have to take lessons?
{{name}}, if you were any more inbred, you’d be a sandwich.
{{name}}, every time I have a stick in my hand, you start to look more and more like a piñata.
{{name}}, you’re not pretty enough to have such an ugly personality.
{{name}}, if you’re going to be two-faced, at least make one of them pretty.
{{name}}, if I were on a deserted island with you and a tin of corned beef, I’d rather eat you and talk to the corned beef.
{{name}}, I don’t dislike you, but if you were drowning, I’d give you a high-five.
{{name}}, did you know your incubator had tinted windows? That explains a lot.
{{name}}, I told my therapist about you. She didn’t believe me.
{{name}}, somewhere, a tree is producing oxygen for you. I’m sorry for it.
{{name}}, all mistakes are fixable—except for you.
{{name}}, you’re the reason the divorce rate is so high.
{{name}}, a glowstick has a brighter future than you.
{{name}}, beauty is only skin deep, but ugly goes clean to the bone.
{{name}}, it would be a great day if you accidentally used a glue stick instead of a Chapstick.
{{name}}, I bet I could remove 90 percent of your good looks with a moist towelette.
{{name}}, I know I make a lot of stupid choices, but hanging out with you was the worst of them all.
{{name}}, I was going to make a joke about your life, but I see life beat me to the punch.
{{name}}, it must be fun to wake up each morning knowing that you are that much closer to achieving your dreams of complete and utter mediocrity.
{{name}}, calling you an idiot would be an insult to stupid people. You’re much worse than that.
{{name}}, it’s a parent’s job to raise their children right. So, looking at you, it’s obvious that yours quit after just one day.
{{name}}, you’re so fat, the photo I took of you last Christmas is still printing.
{{name}}, your birth certificate needs to be rewritten as a letter of apology.
{{name}}, it must be nice to never use your brain.
{{name}}, you have miles to go before you reach mediocre.
{{name}}, I used to believe in evolution until I met you. Now I’m not so sure.
{{name}}, I’d love to stay and chat, but to be honest, I’d rather have open-heart surgery.
{{name}}, I don’t have the time (or enough crayons) to explain this to you.
{{name}}, if I had just one wish, it would be that you step on a LEGO while barefoot today.
{{name}}, you have so many gaps in your teeth that it looks like your tongue is in jail.
{{name}}, do your parents realize they’re living proof that two wrongs don’t make a right?
{{name}}, you need a kiss on the neck from an alligator.
{{name}}, it’s too bad you can’t Photoshop your ugly personality.
{{name}}, congrats on being the top of the bell curve.
{{name}}, I really hope you don’t homeschool your kids.
{{name}}, I hope your wife brings a date to your funeral.
{{name}}, don’t be ashamed of who you are. That’s a job for your parents.
{{name}}, I will slap you so hard that even Google won’t be able to find you.
{{name}}, your family tree must be a cactus because you’re all a bunch of pricks.
{{name}}, how long does it take you to put makeup on your two faces every morning?
{{name}}, I would punch you, but I don’t want to improve your appearance.
{{name}}, you’re the reason I don’t want kids.
{{name}}, there’s no need for you to fear success… you have nothing to worry about.
{{name}}, not to be mean, but I’m guessing you’ll have a sparsely attended funeral.
{{name}}, you can be anything you want in life… except rich, attractive and successful.
{{name}}, how do your friends deal with you every day? Oh yeah, that’s right—you don’t have any!
{{name}}, you aren’t the dumbest person in the world, but you’d better hope they don’t die.
{{name}}, everybody knows that you’re stupid, you thought you could fall in love because you saw a fake Cupid.
{{name}}, twinkle, twinkle, little star, I want to hit you with my car.
{{name}}, if I were a dog and you were a flower, I’d lift my leg up and give you a shower.
